name: Depression Anxiety Stress Scales - 21 Item Version
description: A 21-item self-report instrument measuring the three related negative emotional states of depression, anxiety and stress.
instructions: Please read each statement and select a number 0, 1, 2 or 3 which indicates how much the statement applied to you over the past week. There are no right or wrong answers. Do not spend too much time on any statement.

subscales.name: Depression
subscales.description: Measures dysphoria, hopelessness, devaluation of life, self-deprecation, lack of interest/involvement, anhedonia, and inertia

subscales.name: Anxiety
subscales.description: Measures autonomic arousal, skeletal muscle effects, situational anxiety, and subjective experience of anxious affect

subscales.name: Stress
subscales.description: Measures difficulty relaxing, nervous arousal, being easily upset/agitated, irritable/over-reactive and impatient

questions.label: I found it hard to wind down

Applied to me to some degree, or some of the time
Applied to me to a considerable degree or a good part of time
Applied to me very much or most of the time

scoring.note: Scores for each subscale are calculated by summing the scores for the relevant items. Final subscale scores should be multiplied by 2 to match the full DASS-42 norms.

severityBands.label: Normal
severityBands.description: Within normal range across all three domains

riskRules.message: Severe to extremely severe depression symptoms detected
riskRules.recommendation: Immediate clinical evaluation recommended. Consider referral to mental health specialist.

riskRules.message: Severe to extremely severe anxiety symptoms detected
riskRules.recommendation: Clinical evaluation recommended. Consider referral for anxiety treatment.

riskRules.message: Severe to extremely severe stress levels detected
Patient indicates life feels meaningless frequently or more
riskRules.recommendation: Screen for suicidal ideation. Consider immediate safety assessment.

interpretation.clinicalNotes: The DASS-21 is a quantitative measure of distress along the three axes of depression, anxiety and stress. It is not a categorical measure of clinical diagnoses. Recommended cutoffs are based on score distributions in normative samples.

actions.normal: No intervention required; continue monitoring if clinically indicated
actions.mild: Suggest self-help strategies; monitor at follow-up
actions.moderate: Consider psychological intervention; may benefit from treatment
actions.extremely-severe: Urgent psychological or psychiatric intervention recommended

references: Lovibond, S.H. & Lovibond, P.F. (1995). Manual for the Depression Anxiety Stress Scales (2nd ed.). Sydney: Psychology Foundation.
Antony, M.M., et al. (1998). Psychometric properties of the 42-item and 21-item versions of the DASS. Behaviour Research and Therapy, 36, 167-179.
